protocol.name: linux_dmabuf_unstable_v1

interface.name: zwp_linux_dmabuf_v1
interface.description:
Following the interfaces from: https://www.khronos.org/registry/egl/extensions/EXT/EGL_EXT_image_dma_buf_import.txt https://www.khronos.org/registry/EGL/extensions/EXT/EGL_EXT_image_dma_buf_import_modifiers.txt and the Linux DRM sub-system's AddFb2 ioctl.
This interface offers ways to create generic dmabuf-based wl_buffers.

request.name: destroy
request.description: Objects created through this interface, especially wl_buffers, will remain valid.

request.name: create_params

description.summary: create a temporary object for buffer parameters

request.description: This temporary object is used to collect multiple dmabuf handles into a single batch to create a wl_buffer. It can only be used once and should be destroyed after a 'created' or 'failed' event has been received.

event.name: format
event.description: This event advertises one buffer format that the server supports.

event.name: modifier
event.description: This event advertises the formats that the server supports, along with the modifiers supported for each format.

request.name: get_default_feedback
request.description: This request creates a new wp_linux_dmabuf_feedback object not bound to a particular surface.

request.name: get_surface_feedback
request.description: This request creates a new wp_linux_dmabuf_feedback object for the specified wl_surface.

interface.name: zwp_linux_buffer_params_v1

description.summary: parameters for creating a dmabuf-based wl_buffer

interface.description: This temporary object is a collection of dmabufs and other parameters that together form a single logical buffer.

entry.name: already_used
entry.summary: the dmabuf_batch object has already been used to create a wl_buffer

entry.name: plane_set
entry.summary: the plane index was already set

entry.name: incomplete
entry.summary: missing or too many planes to create a buffer

entry.name: out_of_bounds
entry.summary: offset + stride * height goes out of dmabuf bounds

entry.name: invalid_wl_buffer
entry.summary: invalid wl_buffer resulted from importing dmabufs via the create_immed request on given buffer_params

request.name: destroy

description.summary: delete this object, used or not

request.description: Cleans up the temporary data sent to the server for dmabuf-based wl_buffer creation.

request.name: add

description.summary: add a dmabuf to the temporary set

request.description: This request adds one dmabuf to the set in this zwp_linux_buffer_params_v1.

request.name: create

description.summary: create a wl_buffer from the given dmabufs

request.description: This asks for creation of a wl_buffer from the added dmabuf buffers.

event.name: created
event.description: This event indicates that the attempted buffer creation was successful.

event.name: failed
event.description: This event indicates that the attempted buffer creation has failed.

request.name: create_immed

description.summary: immediately create a wl_buffer from the given dmabufs

request.description: This asks for immediate creation of a wl_buffer by importing the added dmabufs.

arg.name: buffer_id
arg.summary: id for the newly created wl_buffer

interface.name: zwp_linux_dmabuf_feedback_v1
interface.description: This object advertises dmabuf parameters feedback.

request.name: destroy
request.description: Using this request a client can tell the server that it is not going to use the wp_linux_dmabuf_feedback object anymore.

event.name: done
event.description: This event is sent after all parameters of a wp_linux_dmabuf_feedback object have been sent.

event.name: format_table
event.description: This event provides a file descriptor which can be memory-mapped to access the format and modifier table.

event.name: main_device
event.description: This event advertises the main device that the server prefers to use.

event.name: tranche_done

description.summary: a preference tranche has been sent

event.description: This event splits tranche_target_device and tranche_formats events in preference tranches.

event.name: tranche_target_device
event.description: This event advertises the target device that the server prefers to use for a buffer created given this tranche.

event.name: tranche_formats
event.description: This event advertises the format + modifier combinations that the compositor supports.
